section.heading: Environmental health program

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 2 years after December 19, 2007, the Administrator, in consultation with the Secretary of Education, the Secretary of Health and Human Services, and other relevant agencies, shall issue voluntary guidelines for use by the State in developing and implementing an environmental health program for schools that—

paragraph.chapeau: takes into account the status and findings of Federal initiatives established under this subchapter or subtitle C of title IV of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 [42 U.S.C. 17091 et seq.] and other relevant Federal law with respect to school facilities, including relevant updates on trends in the field, such as the impact of school facility environments on student and staff—

paragraph.content: takes into account studies using relevant tools identified or developed in accordance with section 492 of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 [42 U.S.C. 17122];

paragraph.chapeau: takes into account, with respect to school facilities, each of—

subparagraph.chapeau: environmental problems, contaminants, hazardous substances, and pollutant emissions, including—

clause.content: the presence of elemental mercury releases from products and containers;
pollutant emissions from materials and products; and
any other environmental problem, contaminant, hazardous substance, or pollutant emission that present or may present a risk to the health of occupants of the school facilities or environment;

subparagraph.content: heating and cooling choices and technologies;
maintenance, cleaning, and pest control activities;
other issues relating to the health, comfort, productivity, and performance of occupants of the school facilities;

paragraph.content: provides technical assistance on siting, design, management, and operation of school facilities, including facilities used by students with disabilities or special needs;
collaborates with federally funded pediatric environmental health centers to assist in on-site school environmental investigations;
assists States and the public in better understanding and improving the environmental health of children; and
takes into account the special vulnerability of children in low-income and minority communities to exposures from contaminants, hazardous substances, and pollutant emissions.

subsection.heading: Public outreach
subsection.chapeau: The Federal Director and Commercial Director shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that the public clearinghouse established under section 423 of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 [42 U.S.C. 17083] receives and makes available—

paragraph.content: information from the Administrator that is contained in the report described in section 2695b(a) of this title; and
information on the exposure of children to environmental hazards in school facilities, as provided by the Administrator.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 94–469, title V, § 504, as added Pub. L. 110–140, title IV, § 461(a), Dec. 19, 2007, 121 Stat. 1641.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), is Pub. L. 110–140, Dec. 19, 2007, 121 Stat. 1492. Subtitle C of title IV of the Act enacted part C (§ 17091 et seq.) of subchapter III of chapter 152 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, amended sections 6832, 6834, 8253, and 8254 of Title 42, and enacted provisions set out as a note under section 6834 of Title 42. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 17001 of Title 42 and Tables.
